title: Executing the Training Pass
concept: Backpropagating sentiment loss through time (BPTT) and optimizing weight parameters.

exercises.prompt: What do we calculate to measure how well our sentiment analyzer is performing?
exercises.explain: Sentiment loss helps us understand how accurate our sentiment analyzer's predictions are compared to the real labels.

cards.title: What is Backpropagation?
cards.body: Backpropagation is a method used to improve a model by adjusting its internal settings based on the mistakes it made.

cards.title: Understanding Sentiment Loss
cards.body: Sentiment loss measures how far off our model's predictions are from the true labels. Lower loss means better accuracy!

cards.title: Training Like a Teacher
cards.body: Imagine a teacher gives feedback on assignments. The teacher (backpropagation) helps students (model) learn from mistakes (loss) to improve for next time.

cards.title: Loss Calculation Example
cards.body: If our model predicted a positive sentiment when it was actually negative, the sentiment loss will be higher, signaling that we need to adjust!

cards.title: Myth: More Layers Mean Always Better Performance
cards.body: More layers can actually lead to overfitting. It’s important to find a balance rather than just piling on layers.

cards.title: Process of Backpropagation
cards.body: Backpropagation is like a feedback loop. It moves backwards through the network adjusting weights based on calculated loss.

exercises.explain: During backpropagation, we adjust weight parameters to minimize sentiment loss and improve our model's accuracy.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Backpropagation only happens once during training.
exercises.explain: Backpropagation happens multiple times during training as the model learns from each batch of data.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Backpropagation helps in adjusting ______ parameters to minimize loss.
exercises.sentence: Backpropagation helps in adjusting weight parameters to minimize loss.
exercises.explain: The correct term, 'weight parameters,' refers to the internal values that influence predictions in the model.

exercises.prompt: Which option shows what happens during training with backpropagation?

options.text: Weights are adjusted to reduce loss.
No feedback is given to the model.

exercises.explain: During training, backpropagation adjusts weights based on the loss to optimize the model's performance.

exercises.prompt: What is the goal of backpropagation in optimizing a sentiment analyzer?

options.text: To adjust weights based on calculated loss

exercises.explain: The goal of backpropagation is to adjust the weights based on calculated loss, helping the model learn from its mistakes and improve accuracy.
